metadata.notes: Each template provides a structured outline for task training per Part 46 requirements. Actual training content must be customized to site-specific equipment and procedures.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely isolate energy sources, apply locks and tags, verify zero energy state, and restore equipment to service following the site LOTO procedure
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
Assigned as authorized LOTO employee by supervisor
training_steps: Review the site-specific LOTO written procedure and regulatory basis (30 CFR 56.12016)
Identify all energy types: electrical, mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal, chemical, gravitational
Demonstrate how to locate and interpret equipment-specific LOTO procedures (posted or in binder)
Walk through the sequence: notify affected employees, shut down equipment, isolate energy sources, apply lock and tag, verify zero energy
Practice applying individual lock with personal tag on a designated training station or actual equipment under supervision
Demonstrate group LOTO procedures: each person applies own lock, last person to remove lock verifies area is clear
Explain the lock removal process: only the lock owner removes their lock, shift-change protocol, absent employee lock removal (supervisor authorization)
Practice restoring equipment to service: remove tools, replace guards, remove locks in sequence, notify affected employees, restart
hazards: Unexpected energization causing amputation, electrocution, or crushing
Stored energy release (pressurized lines, raised loads, capacitors, springs)
Incorrect isolation point due to mislabeled equipment or outdated procedures
Failure to verify zero energy after applying locks

competency_evaluation.method: Hands-on demonstration with competent person observation
criteria: Correctly identifies all energy sources for a given piece of equipment
Follows the written LOTO procedure in correct sequence without prompting
Applies personal lock and tag correctly with required information on tag
Successfully verifies zero energy state using approved method
Correctly explains group LOTO and shift-change procedures
Demonstrates equipment restart procedure including guard replacement and notification
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must demonstrate all criteria correctly. Any missed step requires re-training on that step and re-evaluation.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely identify permit-required confined spaces, perform atmospheric testing, and conduct entry following the site confined space entry procedure
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
LOTO training (TT-001) completed
Current first aid/CPR certification recommended
ppe_required: Full body harness with retrieval D-ring
Supplied air respirator or SCBA (as required by permit)
training_steps: Define confined space and permit-required confined space per 30 CFR 56.5002 and site procedure
Review site confined space inventory and identify spaces relevant to trainee's work area (silos, mills, tanks, hoppers, ducts)
Demonstrate atmospheric testing: calibration of 4-gas monitor, testing sequence (O2, LEL, CO, H2S), acceptable entry levels, continuous monitoring requirements
Walk through the permit process: entry permit completion, authorized entrant/attendant/entry supervisor roles, pre-entry checklist
Demonstrate LOTO of all energy sources associated with the confined space (feeds, drives, agitators, piping)
Practice ventilation setup: forced air ventilation, duct placement, airflow verification
Review rescue plan: on-site rescue team or 911, retrieval system setup, non-entry rescue preference, attendant communication
Conduct a simulated entry (or actual supervised entry if available) with full permit, testing, and communication protocols
hazards: Oxygen-deficient or oxygen-enriched atmosphere
Toxic gas accumulation (CO from incomplete combustion, H2S from organic material, SO2 in kiln-related spaces)
Engulfment by stored material (clinker, raw meal, cement)
Entrapment by internal configuration (baffles, converging walls)
Thermal hazards in recently operated equipment (kilns, mills, preheater cyclones)

competency_evaluation.method: Practical demonstration of confined space entry setup and atmospheric testing
criteria: Correctly identifies permit-required confined spaces in assigned work area
Calibrates and operates 4-gas monitor, interprets readings, identifies unacceptable conditions
Completes entry permit accurately with all required fields
Demonstrates correct LOTO of confined space energy sources
Sets up ventilation and verifies adequate airflow before entry
Explains rescue plan and demonstrates retrieval system connection
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must demonstrate all criteria. Atmospheric testing and LOTO competency are critical and require 100% accuracy.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely perform or oversee hot work (welding, cutting, grinding) by following the hot work permit process and fire prevention requirements
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
Fire extinguisher training
ppe_required: Welding helmet or cutting goggles (appropriate shade)
Respiratory protection if in confined or poorly ventilated area
training_steps: Review the hot work permit process and regulatory basis (30 CFR 56.4100 series)
Identify hot work activities: welding, torch cutting, brazing, grinding, any spark-producing work
Demonstrate the pre-work fire hazard assessment: 35-foot radius inspection, combustible material removal or covering, checking floors/walls/ceilings for fire paths
Complete a hot work permit with all required fields: location, work description, fire prevention measures, start/stop times, signatures
Assign and brief the fire watch: duties, location, communication, duration (minimum 30 minutes after work completion), fire extinguisher readiness
Demonstrate fire extinguisher operation (PASS method) and location of nearest extinguishers and pull stations
Review special conditions: hot work near conveyors (belt fire risk), near fuel/oil storage, in confined spaces (additional ventilation and permit), on equipment containing residual flammable material
Close out the permit: fire watch sign-off, area re-inspection, permit filing
hazards: Fire ignition from sparks, slag, or hot metal contacting combustibles
Toxic fume inhalation (galvanized steel produces zinc oxide fumes, stainless steel produces chromium/nickel fumes)
UV radiation burns to eyes and skin
Explosion risk if hot work near flammable vapors, dust, or containers
Burns from hot metal, slag, and spatter

competency_evaluation.method: Supervised completion of hot work permit and fire hazard assessment
criteria: Correctly assesses the work area for fire hazards within 35-foot radius
Completes hot work permit with all required information
Briefs fire watch on duties, duration, and communication
Identifies when additional precautions are needed (confined space, flammable materials)
Demonstrates fire extinguisher readiness and operation
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must complete all steps accurately. Fire watch briefing and hazard assessment are pass/fail items.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely start, stop, and perform routine walking inspection of conveyor systems following site procedures
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
LOTO training (TT-001) completed
ppe_required: Dust mask or respirator in dusty transfer areas
training_steps: Review conveyor system layout: identify head pulleys, tail pulleys, bend pulleys, take-ups, drives, transfer points, and emergency pull cords for the trainee's assigned conveyor(s)
Demonstrate the pre-start checklist: walk the entire conveyor length, verify no personnel on or near the belt, check that all guards are in place, verify pull cords are functional and reset
Explain the startup warning system: audible alarm duration (minimum 10 seconds per site procedure), visual warning lights, DCS or local start protocols
Demonstrate normal start and stop from local control panel and from DCS (control room)
Practice emergency stop: pull cord activation, E-stop button, and response procedure after emergency stop (do not restart until cause is investigated)
Walk through routine inspection points: belt tracking, splice condition, idler rotation and noise, material spillage, pulley lagging, take-up travel, structure integrity
Review common conveyor hazards: nip points at pulleys, material falling from return side, belt tracking off causing rub points, hot material (clinker conveyors)
Demonstrate proper housekeeping: spillage cleanup methods, safe access to clean under conveyors, never clean while belt is running
hazards: Entanglement at nip points (pulleys, idlers, return rollers) causing amputation or death
Struck by falling material from belt or transfer chutes
Engulfment if entering hopper or chute area without LOTO
Noise exposure above 90 dBA at drive areas and transfer points
Dust exposure at transfer points, especially with high-silica raw materials

competency_evaluation.method: Observed pre-start walk, startup, shutdown, and inspection of assigned conveyor
criteria: Completes pre-start walking inspection identifying all checkpoints
Verifies guards are in place and pull cords are functional before authorizing start
Follows correct startup sequence including warning alarm
Demonstrates emergency stop and explains post-stop investigation procedure
Identifies at least 5 inspection items during walking inspection
Maintains safe distance from nip points at all times during inspection
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must demonstrate all items. Failure to check pull cords or maintain safe distance from nip points requires immediate re-training.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the operator to safely perform a kiln area observation round, identify normal and abnormal conditions, and report findings
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
Basic kiln process awareness
Gas hazard awareness training
ppe_required: Safety glasses with side shields (tinted for viewing through kiln peepholes)
Long-sleeve shirt and pants (no synthetic materials in kiln area)
training_steps: Review the kiln observation route map and standard operating procedure for observation rounds
Explain kiln process basics relevant to safety: rotation direction, normal shell temperatures, burner operation, preheater gas flow, clinker cooler function
Demonstrate use of portable gas monitor: bump test before round, alarm set points (CO 35 ppm, SO2 2 ppm), response to alarm (evacuate upwind, report to control room)
Walk the observation route: preheater tower (top down, noting access restrictions), kiln feed end, kiln shell (shell scanner correlation, hot spots), kiln drive area, burner platform, clinker cooler
Demonstrate kiln shell temperature observation: compare to shell scanner data, identify red/hot spots indicating refractory loss, reporting threshold
Explain rotating equipment hazards: safe distance from kiln tires and rollers, support roller bearing checks (visual, temperature, vibration), thrust roller area exclusion
Review kiln burner area: safe viewing through peepholes, signs of abnormal flame, clinker ring formation, dust return
Complete the observation checklist and demonstrate radio communication protocol with control room for reporting abnormal findings
hazards: Thermal burns from kiln shell (surface temperatures up to 700F where refractory is thin or gone)
CO and SO2 gas exposure, particularly in preheater tower and near kiln seals
Rotating equipment entanglement at kiln tires, rollers, and drive gears
Falls from height in preheater tower (up to 400 ft) and on kiln platforms
Noise exposure exceeding 100 dBA near burner and cooler areas
Clinker dust and hot clinker contact at cooler discharge

competency_evaluation.method: Accompanied observation round with competent person scoring checklist completion and hazard recognition
criteria: Correctly uses and interprets portable gas monitor readings
Follows observation route in correct sequence, accessing all checkpoints safely
Identifies normal vs abnormal kiln shell temperatures and explains reporting threshold
Maintains safe distance from rotating equipment and hot surfaces throughout round
Correctly communicates findings to control room via radio using standard terminology
Completes observation checklist accurately and completely
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must complete full round with zero safety deviations. Gas monitor use and rotating equipment awareness are critical pass/fail items.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely operate a forklift (sit-down counterbalance type) for material handling in warehouse, packing, and shipping areas
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
Valid driver's license recommended
Medical clearance if required by site policy
ppe_required: Seatbelt (used at all times while operating)
training_steps: Review forklift types on site and the specific model(s) to be operated, including capacity ratings and load charts
Demonstrate pre-shift inspection: fluid levels, tire condition, forks (cracks, wear), mast and chains, hydraulics, lights, horn, backup alarm, seatbelt, fire extinguisher
Explain stability triangle, center of gravity, and tip-over prevention: no turning on grades, slow speed on turns, forks low during travel, no elevated loads during travel
Demonstrate basic operations: starting, forward/reverse, steering, lifting, lowering, tilting, horn use at intersections and blind spots
Practice load handling: approach load squarely, fork insertion to full depth, tilt back before lifting, travel with load low (4-6 inches), stacking and destacking on racking
Review pedestrian safety: horn at intersections, stop at blind corners, right-of-way rules, no riders on forks, pedestrian exclusion zones during loading
Demonstrate refueling/recharging procedures: propane tank change or battery charging area safety, ventilation, no smoking, spill response
Practice parking: forks flat on ground, mast tilted forward, parking brake set, key removed, park in designated area only
hazards: Tip-over causing crushing injury or death to operator
Pedestrian struck by forklift or falling load
Load falling from forks due to improper stacking, overloading, or traveling with raised load
Collision with racking, structures, or other vehicles
Propane leak or battery acid exposure during refueling/recharging

competency_evaluation.method: Observed pre-shift inspection and practical driving/load handling course
criteria: Completes pre-shift inspection identifying all required checkpoints and documenting results
Demonstrates smooth forward and reverse operation with appropriate speed control
Correctly picks up, transports, and places loads at multiple heights without instability
Uses horn at all intersections and blind spots, scans for pedestrians before moving
Parks forklift correctly with forks down, brake set, and key removed
Explains tip-over prevention and what to do if tip-over occurs (stay in, brace, lean away from ground)
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must pass all practical items. Any unsafe act during evaluation requires full re-training before re-evaluation.

task_training_templates.task_name: Overhead Crane Operation and Rigging Signals
task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely operate an overhead bridge crane and/or serve as a signal person for crane operations in the plant
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
LOTO training recommended
ppe_required: High-visibility vest when serving as signal person
training_steps: Review crane types on site (overhead bridge, jib, gantry), rated capacities, and load charts

Demonstrate pre-use inspection: wire rope condition (broken wires, kinking, corrosion), hooks (deformation, safety latch), limit switches, brakes, pendant or radio controls, runway and bridge travel paths
Explain load weight estimation: use load charts, weigh if uncertain, never exceed rated capacity, account for rigging weight, understand dynamic loading
Demonstrate standard hand signals per ASME B30.5: hoist, lower, bridge travel, trolley travel, stop, emergency stop, dog everything
Practice basic lifts: attach sling correctly (choker, basket, vertical hitch), ensure load is balanced, lift slowly to test balance, travel with load as low as possible, never travel over personnel
Review rigging hardware: slings (wire rope, chain, synthetic), shackles, eyebolts, spreader bars; inspection criteria and rejection; proper sling angles (never below 30 degrees)
Demonstrate communication protocol: one designated signal person, eye contact with operator before lift, stop means stop from anyone
Practice setting loads: guide with tag lines (never hands), lower slowly, verify stability before releasing rigging, clear area before slack
hazards: Dropped load causing crushing injury or death
Crane structural failure from overloading
Electrocution from contact with overhead power lines (outdoor cranes) or energized equipment
Swing or drift of suspended load striking personnel
Rigging failure from improper sling selection, damaged hardware, or incorrect hitch
Pinch points between load and structure during placement

competency_evaluation.method: Observed pre-use inspection, rigging setup, and supervised lift sequence
criteria: Completes pre-use inspection and correctly identifies accept/reject conditions on ropes, hooks, and hardware
Demonstrates all standard hand signals correctly
Selects appropriate rigging for a given load weight and configuration
Performs a test lift, confirms load balance, and transports load safely to destination
Maintains load path clear of personnel at all times
Sets load down using tag lines, verifies stability, and removes rigging safely
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must demonstrate all items. Overloading the crane or traveling a load over personnel is an automatic failure requiring full re-training.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely charge grinding media (steel balls or cylpebs) into a ball mill using the approved method and equipment
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
LOTO training (TT-001) completed
Overhead crane or front-end loader training (depending on charging method)
training_steps: Review the media charging procedure and schedule: media type and size for each compartment, quantity per charge, charging frequency
Explain the LOTO requirements: full isolation of mill drive, auxiliary drive, separator, and feed system; verification of zero energy; mill must be stationary and locked
Demonstrate media delivery and staging: forklift or crane transport of media containers/super sacks to charging area, secure placement, no stacking above eye level
Walk through the charging method: manual charging via feed chute, mechanical conveyor charging, or crane with media bucket; site-specific method
Demonstrate safe pouring/placement technique: controlled rate to avoid bridging at inlet, keep body clear of chute opening, never reach into chute while media is flowing
Review pinch point and struck-by hazards: heavy media containers, rolling balls, chute openings, overhead crane movements during charging
Explain noise exposure management: charging generates extreme noise levels (>110 dBA); limit exposure time; double hearing protection mandatory; communication via hand signals during charging
Demonstrate post-charging: secure mill inlet, verify all tools and materials removed, close and secure access doors, remove LOTO in correct sequence, document media addition in log
hazards: Struck by falling or rolling grinding media (steel balls weigh 2-15 lbs each)
Crushing injury from media container handling (super sacks weigh 1-2 tons)
Noise-induced hearing loss from extreme noise during charging
Entanglement or caught-between if mill rotates during charging (LOTO failure)
Fall hazard on media spilled on floor or platform

competency_evaluation.method: Observed media charging operation under supervision of competent person
criteria: Verifies full LOTO of mill and associated equipment before beginning
Stages media containers safely using approved material handling equipment
Follows charging procedure in correct sequence without exceeding prescribed quantity
Wears all required PPE including double hearing protection throughout the operation
Maintains safe body position away from chute opening and pinch points
Completes post-charging inspection and documentation
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must demonstrate all criteria. LOTO verification and PPE compliance are pass/fail critical items.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee on safety requirements for working in or overseeing refractory installation and demolition activities inside kilns, preheater cyclones, and other high-temperature vessels
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
Confined space entry training (TT-002) completed
Fall protection training
LOTO training (TT-001) completed
ppe_required: Full body harness with shock-absorbing lanyard (for work inside kiln on scaffolding)
P100 respirator (for refractory demolition dust, including silica)
training_steps: Review the scope of refractory work: kiln brick lining replacement, preheater castable repair, cooler refractory patching; each has different hazard profiles
Explain the LOTO and confined space requirements: full kiln LOTO (main drive, auxiliary drive, feed, fuel), continuous atmospheric monitoring, forced ventilation, rescue plan
Demonstrate scaffolding safety inside the kiln: scaffold must be purpose-built for kiln interior (curved base), inspection before each use, fall protection tie-off points, maximum working height

Review silica dust exposure: refractory bricks and castable contain up to 90% silica; demolition creates extreme respirable dust; P100 or supplied-air respirator required; wet methods when feasible; personal dust monitoring

Explain thermal hazards: kiln shell may still be hot after cool-down (allow minimum 24-48 hours per site procedure); check surface temperatures before entry; castable generates heat during curing (exothermic reaction)
Demonstrate proper material handling: bricks weigh 20-50 lbs each, use mechanical aids for transport, staging area organization, no throwing bricks, bucket lifts for debris removal
Review chemical hazards: some refractory contains chrome oxide (hexavalent chromium when heated); castable cement is causite and causes chemical burns; skin protection and washing requirements
Explain the permit and oversight structure: hot work permit if torch cutting anchors, confined space permit, daily safety briefing, contractor safety coordination
hazards: Silica dust exposure causing silicosis (refractory demolition is one of the highest-exposure tasks in a cement plant)
Falls from scaffolding inside kiln (up to 15 ft depending on kiln diameter)
Thermal burns from hot kiln shell, residual heat, or exothermic castable curing
Struck by falling bricks or debris during demolition
Musculoskeletal injuries from repetitive heavy lifting of refractory bricks
Chemical burns from wet castable (pH > 12)

competency_evaluation.method: Written quiz on refractory hazards plus observed demonstration of PPE donning and confined space entry procedures for kiln work
criteria: Correctly identifies the three primary hazards of refractory work (silica dust, falls, thermal)
Demonstrates proper respirator fit check and explains cartridge/filter selection for silica
Explains the LOTO and confined space requirements specific to kiln entry
Demonstrates harness donning and identifies proper anchor points on kiln scaffolding
Identifies chrome-containing refractory and explains special handling requirements
Explains the emergency rescue plan for personnel inside the kiln
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must pass written quiz with 80% or higher AND demonstrate all practical items. Respirator fit and fall protection are pass/fail critical items.

task_training_templates.objective: Train the employee to safely receive, store, handle, and dose water treatment chemicals used in plant cooling systems, boiler water treatment, and dust suppression systems
prerequisites: New miner or experienced miner training completed
Hazard communication / GHS training
ppe_required: Face shield (for transfer and mixing operations)
Chemical-resistant gloves (nitrile or neoprene per SDS)
training_steps: Review the chemicals used on site: common water treatment chemicals include sulfuric acid, sodium hypochlorite, anti-scalants, corrosion inhibitors, biocides, pH adjusters; identify those in trainee's area
Demonstrate SDS access and interpretation: locate SDS binder or electronic system, identify key sections (Section 2: hazards, Section 4: first aid, Section 7: handling/storage, Section 8: PPE)
Explain chemical labeling: GHS labels, pictograms, signal words (Danger vs Warning), secondary container labeling requirements
Demonstrate chemical receiving: inspect containers for damage, verify correct chemical against PO and SDS, spill prevention during unloading, connection of bulk delivery hoses
Walk through storage requirements: incompatible chemical separation (acids away from hypochlorite), secondary containment, ventilation, temperature limits, shelf life tracking
Demonstrate dosing system operation: pump calibration verification, line inspection for leaks, chemical day tank filling procedure, manual dosing procedure if automated system is down
Review spill response: small spill containment and cleanup (absorbent, neutralizer), large spill evacuation and reporting, spill kit locations, never wash chemicals into storm drains
Demonstrate emergency procedures: eyewash and safety shower use (15-minute flush minimum for chemical splash), first aid for skin and eye contact, when to call emergency services vs handle on site
hazards: Chemical burns from skin or eye contact with concentrated acids or caustics
Toxic gas generation from mixing incompatible chemicals (e.g., acid + hypochlorite generates chlorine gas)
Inhalation of chemical vapors or mists during transfer and dosing
Slip hazard from chemical spills on floors and platforms
Environmental release to storm drains or process water

competency_evaluation.method: Observed chemical handling demonstration and SDS interpretation quiz
criteria: Locates and correctly interprets SDS for the chemicals in their work area
Selects and dons correct PPE for the specific chemicals being handled
Demonstrates safe transfer/dosing procedure without spills
Identifies incompatible chemicals in the storage area and explains separation requirements
Demonstrates correct spill response including containment and reporting
Uses emergency eyewash station correctly and explains the 15-minute flush requirement
competency_evaluation.passing_standard: Must demonstrate all criteria. PPE selection and incompatible chemical identification are pass/fail critical items.
